app.name: WPPConnect
app.one_liner: An open-source Node.js library that exports WhatsApp Web functionality to enable automation, chatbots, and AI-driven messaging interactions.

screens.name: WhatsApp Automation Dashboard
screens.purpose: Provide developers with an entry point to configure sessions, send messages, and monitor WhatsApp interactions.

rust_commands.name: fetch_contacts
rust_commands.purpose: Retrieve user's contact list, group memberships, and block list from WhatsApp.

rust_commands.name: send_message
rust_commands.purpose: Send text, image, video, audio, documents, contacts, stickers, or location via WhatsApp.

rust_commands.name: start_session
rust_commands.purpose: Initialize a new WhatsApp Web session using QR code authentication.

mvp_plan: week 1: Expose basic message sending and contact fetching APIs
week 1: Implement core session management using WhatsApp Web reverse engineering
week 1: Set up TypeScript project structure with linting and CI
week 2: Add support for media types (image, video, audio, docs)
week 2: Integrate external documentation links and getting-started guides
week 3: Add location sharing and sticker support
week 3: Implement message forwarding and receipt handling
week 3: Publish initial version with dependency tracking for WhatsApp Web changes
acceptance_tests: Application successfully starts a WhatsApp session and refreshes QR code automatically.
Contact list, group memberships, and block list are retrievable via API.
Multiple concurrent sessions operate without interference.
System warns user when package is outdated relative to WhatsApp Web changes.
User can send text, image, and video messages to individual contacts and groups.
